captures.notes: Canonical article link for attribution and TSL/WebGPU mental-model framing; local evidence below is generated from scratch for learning review.
Local generated rebuild screenshot showing shader nodes, uniform chips, material passes, compute lanes, and compile flow; not copied from the article.
Local generated motion clip isolates node activation, material compile flow, and compute-lane state; not copied from the article.
Standalone local HTML/SVG exercise reconstructing the primitive with generated nodes, uniforms, passes, compute lanes, and CSS-only compile motion.
Manifest lists only generated local learning evidence plus source-link attribution boundaries.

payload.learning_primitive: TSL shader graph as material grammar
payload.visual_role: teaches how a contemporary WebGPU material is authored as readable node logic before it becomes surface style

modules.review_notes: Complete for first-pass learning: still image, motion clip, and code note all show the same TSL material primitive.

payload.compute_model: lanes represent dispatched work feeding material state before render

modules.review_notes: Performance layer is complete as a teaching abstraction, not a benchmark of the original article examples.

payload.evidence_layer: local PNG proves node/uniform/pass taxonomy, local GIF proves compile motion, HTML proves the shader-material rebuild recipe
payload.learning_primitive: review transformation graph as shader material
payload.review_gap: canonical article remains link-only; local rebuild teaches mechanics without article screenshots, code snippets, shaders, demos, or media
payload.route_decision: use nodes for claim transformation, uniforms for reviewer controls, passes for evidence styling stages, and compute lanes for background review work
payload.source_trail: canonical Maxime Heckel article plus generated local reconstruction
payload.uncertainty: exact article examples, source code, diagrams, and benchmark behavior are not claimed

modules.review_notes: VULCA mapping is complete because source, evidence, review gap, route decision, and uncertainty are all represented.

summary: TSL/WebGPU teaching reference rebuilt as a local shader-material primitive with node graph, uniforms, material passes, compute lanes, motion, code, and asset evidence.
title: Field Guide to TSL and WebGPU
why_relevant: Completes the atlas's lesson-path branch for contemporary shader materials by making node composition, compile flow, and runtime budget visible.
